title: MEDIUM: Card metadata not sanitized before DB storage
description:
**Category: HTML Sanitization (3)**

In `card.rs:180-195`, OG metadata fields (`title`, `description`, `site_name`) parsed from remote web pages are stored in the database without HTML sanitization. The `parse_og_meta` function extracts raw content attribute values and runs them through `html_decode` (which converts entities back to literal characters), but does not sanitize.

Currently, card data is only serialized as JSON in ActivityPub Note responses (`get_card_for_post`), where it goes through serde_json's string encoding. However:

1. If card data is ever rendered in an HTML context (e.g., link preview cards on the profile page), it would be an XSS vector.
2. The `html_decode` function (card.rs:120-127) actively reverses entity encoding (`<` → `<`, `&` → `&`), converting safe encoded values back to potentially dangerous literal characters.

**Fix:** Run card metadata through `sanitize::html_to_text()` (strips all HTML) before storage, or at minimum apply length caps:
- title: 512 chars
- description: 2048 chars
- site_name: 256 chars
close_reason: Card metadata (title, description, site_name) now stripped of HTML via html_to_text() before DB storage, plus length caps (512/2048/256). html_decode output is sanitized before insert.
